skill.name: app-store-changelog
skill.description: Generate user-facing App Store release notes from git history, tags, changed files, and release-note writing guidelines.
skill.summary: Turn git history into concise App Store release notes.
skill.author: Dimillian

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported high-risk weak cryptography and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are keyword false positives in changelog guidance and documentation. The real risk is limited to a local shell helper that runs read-only git commands and prints repository history, so publication is reasonable with normal script-use caution.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography matches occur in release-note prose, markdown metadata, or git tag discovery. No cryptographic algorithm implementation or insecure hash use is present at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain changelog wording, markdown descriptions, or git tag commands. There is no crypto API, digest calculation, credential handling, or encryption logic.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance lines are writing guidance about avoiding file paths, API jargon, and internal wording in App Store notes. They do not run discovery commands or collect host information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is plain-language release-note guidance. I found no host enumeration, network inventory, or environment probing behavior on these lines.

low_findings.title: Documentation Backtick Command Matches Are Benign
low_findings.description: The markdown backticks document how to run the included helper script and reference its location. They are not Ruby execution, hidden shell execution, or automatic command dispatch by the skill file itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are markdown code spans and resource bullets. They instruct an assistant or user to run a local helper, but the markdown itself does not execute code.

low_findings.title: Standard Device Redirection Is Safe
low_findings.description: The script redirects git tag lookup output to /dev/null only to test whether tags exist. This suppresses command output and does not read secrets or device data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The redirection is attached to git describe and only discards stdout and stderr. No sensitive filesystem path or device content is accessed.

low_findings.title: Local Git Helper Executes Read-Only Commands
low_findings.description: The helper runs local git commands and accepts optional refs. This is expected for the skill, but users should run it only in repositories they intend to inspect because it prints commit subjects, touched files, and the repository root.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly invokes git and prints local repository metadata. The commands are read-only and there is no network or deletion behavior, so the severity remains low.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Git Commands With Caller-Supplied Refs
dangerous_patterns.description: The shell helper builds a git log range from optional command-line refs and passes it to git log. This is legitimate changelog collection, but it is still local command execution over repository metadata.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is intentional and bounded to git history collection. Risk depends on where the script is run and which refs the caller supplies.

content.value_statement: Release teams often need clear App Store notes from messy git history. This skill collects recent changes and turns them into user-facing bullets.
actual_capabilities: Collects commits since the latest git tag or a specified ref.
Lists touched files to help identify user-facing changes.
Filters internal work such as refactors, CI changes, and dependency bumps.
Groups release notes by themes such as New, Improved, and Fixed.
Drafts short App Store-friendly bullets in plain language.
Checks that each bullet maps back to a real change.
limitations: Requires access to a local git repository with useful commit history.
Cannot verify product behavior beyond the repository evidence provided.
May need human clarification for ambiguous commits or internal changes.
Does not publish notes to App Store Connect.

use_cases.title: Prepare Mobile Release Notes
use_cases.description: Convert recent commits into clear What's New bullets before submitting an app update.

use_cases.title: Summarize Engineering Changes
use_cases.description: Review commit history and separate user-visible updates from internal development work.

use_cases.title: Polish Storefront Copy
use_cases.description: Rewrite technical change descriptions into concise language that users can understand.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Release Notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request using the latest tag range.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate App Store release notes for this repository using the latest git tag as the starting point.

prompt_templates.title: Use a Specific Version Range
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a known release range.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate App Store What's New text for changes from v2.4.0 to HEAD. Keep only user-visible changes.

prompt_templates.title: Group Notes by Theme
prompt_templates.scenario: Structured release notes for a larger update.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create release notes grouped into New, Improved, and Fixed. Include only changes that users would notice.

prompt_templates.title: Audit and Refine Draft Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these draft release notes against the git changes. Remove unsupported claims, merge duplicates, and make the language App Store-ready.

output_examples.input: Generate notes for the current release since the last tag.
output: Added account switching from the profile menu.
Improved timeline loading speed on slow connections.
Fixed media attachments not opening in full screen.

output_examples.input: Summarize v1.8.0 to HEAD for App Store Connect.
output_examples.output: A concise What's New list with user-facing bullets, unsupported internal changes removed, and duplicate fixes merged.

output_examples.input: Check whether this draft matches the repository changes.
output_examples.output: A revised note set that keeps supported user-visible changes and flags ambiguous items for clarification.

best_practices: Run the helper from the repository root before drafting notes.
Keep each bullet tied to a specific commit or touched feature area.
Ask for clarification when a commit message does not show user impact.
anti_patterns: Do not include internal ticket IDs, file paths, or developer jargon.
Do not claim fixes or features that are not supported by git history.
Do not publish raw commit subjects as customer-facing release notes.

faq.question: Does this skill publish to App Store Connect?
faq.answer: No. It drafts release-note text that a user can review and publish separately.

faq.question: Can I choose a specific starting tag?
faq.answer: Yes. The helper accepts an optional starting ref and ending ref for the release range.

faq.question: What happens if the repository has no tags?
faq.answer: The helper falls back to the available history for the selected ending ref.

faq.question: Does it include internal engineering work?
faq.answer: It is designed to exclude internal-only refactors, CI changes, and dependency updates.

faq.question: Can it write notes for Android releases too?
faq.answer: Yes, the wording approach can help mobile release notes, although the guidelines target App Store copy.

faq.question: Is the helper script safe to run?
faq.answer: It runs read-only git commands and prints repository metadata. Review it before running in sensitive repositories.
